## Summary: Presets Functionality in Rich Editor Demo

I found the preset-related functionality in the monorepo. Here's the complete overview:

### 1. **Demo Page Location**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/src/pages/PresetsPage.tsx`

This is the main component that implements the presets showcase with:
- **Layout**: Three-section layout (sidebar + main content area)
- **Sidebar**: List of presets with labels and descriptions
- **Main content**: Editor/renderer display with toolbar controls

### 2. **Preset Definition & Data**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/src/fixtures/presets.ts`

Contains the `Preset` interface and preset collection:
```typescript
interface Preset {
  key: string
  label: string
  description: string
  data: SerializedEditorState  // Lexical editor state JSON
}
```

**Current presets** (5 total):
- `note-diary` — Note variant showcase with serif font, drop cap, Chinese classical text
- `markdown-test-preset` — Markdown rendering test cases
- `tldraw-showcase` — Interactive whiteboard canvas extension demo
- `enhanced-renderers` — All renderer packages (code, image, video, linkcard, gallery, mermaid)
- `comprehensive-article` — Complete feature showcase (18+ node types)
- `tech-article` — Technical documentation with KaTeX, code blocks, alerts

### 3. **State Management**
**In PresetsPage.tsx** (lines 16-29):
```typescript
const [selectedKey, setSelectedKey] = useState(presets[0].key)  // Current preset
const [variant, setVariant] = useState<RichEditorVariant>('article')  // article|comment|note
const [mode, setMode] = useState<'edit' | 'readonly'>('edit')  // Edit vs read-only
const [liveState, setLiveState] = useState<SerializedEditorState | null>(null)
```

**Selection logic**:
- Presets are loaded from fixture file
- Selected preset found via `presets.find((p) => p.key === selectedKey)`
- When changing preset, live state resets to trigger re-render with new content

### 4. **UI Controls** (PresetsPage.tsx lines 58-96)
**Toolbar with two groups**:
- **Mode**: Edit / Readonly buttons (lines 62-73)
- **Variant**: Article / Comment / Note buttons (lines 77-95)

**Preset sidebar** (lines 36-54):
- Clickable buttons for each preset
- Active state styling via CSS class `preset-item-active`
- Shows label + description per preset

### 5. **Routing & App Integration**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/src/App.tsx`

- **Routes**: Hash-based navigation (`#editor`, `#nodes`, `#presets`, `#diff`)
- **Navigation** (line 139): "Presets" tab triggers `navigate('presets')`
- **Page state** (lines 41-46): Parsed from `window.location.hash`
- **Component mounting** (line 166): `{currentPage === 'presets' && <PresetsPage />}`

### 6. **Demo App Details**
**Structure**:
- **Location**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/`
- **Dev server**: Port 5188 (Vite)
- **Entry**: `src/main.tsx` → `src/App.tsx`
- **Pages** in `/src/pages/`:
  - `EditorPage.tsx` — Free-form editor
  - `NodeShowcase.tsx` — Individual node type demos
  - `PresetsPage.tsx` — Preset showcase (our focus)
  - `DiffPage.tsx` — Diff viewer

### 7. **Fixture Helpers**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/src/fixtures/helpers.ts`

Builder functions for constructing `SerializedEditorState`:
- `doc()`, `paragraph()`, `text()` — Basic text nodes
- `heading()`, `quote()`, `list()` — Block structures
- `alertQuote()`, `banner()`, `details()` — Special containers
- `image()`, `video()`, `mermaid()`, `katexBlock()` — Media/math
- `mention()`, `footnote()` — Advanced inline nodes
- `FORMAT_*` constants for text styling (BOLD, ITALIC, CODE, etc.)

---

**Key architectural insight**: Presets are static JSON data (Lexical editor states) that can be swapped in/out. The same component (`ShiroEditor` or `ShiroRenderer`) accepts different initial content, with UI controls for testing different variants and edit modes. This allows comprehensive feature testing in a single interface.

    66→                  Edit
    67→                </button>
    68→                <button
    69→                  className={mode === 'readonly' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
    70→                  onClick={() => setMode('readonly')}
    71→                >
    72→                  Readonly
    73→                </button>
    74→              </div>
    75→              <div className="toolbar-group">
    76→                <span className="toolbar-label">Variant</span>
    77→                <button
    78→                  className={variant === 'article' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
    79→                  onClick={() => setVariant('article')}
    80→                >
    81→                  Article
    82→                </button>
    83→                <button
    84→                  className={variant === 'comment' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
    85→                  onClick={() => setVariant('comment')}
    86→                >
    87→                  Comment
    88→                </button>
    89→                <button
    90→                  className={variant === 'note' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
    91→                  onClick={() => setVariant('note')}
    92→                >
    93→                  Note
    94→                </button>
    95→              </div>
    96→            </div>
    97→
    98→            {/* Renderer */}
    99→            <Panel title={selected.label} badge={`${variant} · ${mode}`}>
   100→              {mode === 'edit' ? (
   101→                <ShiroEditor
   102→                  key={`${selected.key}-edit`}
   103→                  initialValue={selected.data}
   104→                  variant={variant}
   105→                  theme={theme}
   106→                  onChange={setLiveState}
   107→                />
   108→              ) : (
   109→                <ShiroRenderer
   110→                  key={`${selected.key}-readonly`}
   111→                  value={selected.data}
   112→                  variant={variant}
   113→                  theme={theme}
   114→                />
   115→              )}
   116→            </Panel>
   117→
   118→            {/* JSON */}
   119→            <Panel title="Serialized JSON">
   120→              <JsonViewer data={jsonData} defaultExpanded={false} />
   121→            </Panel>
   122→          </div>
   123→        </div>
   124→      </div>
   125→    </MentionPlatformProvider>
   126→  )
   127→}
   128→
```
</details></tool-use>

   235→
   236→async function fetchFeed(cursor?: string): Promise<FeedResponse> {
   237→  const url = new URL('/api/feed', location.origin)
   238→  if (cursor) {

   240→  }
   241→
   242→  const response = await fetch(url.toString())
   243→  if (!response.ok) {

   245→  }
   246→
   247→  return (await response.json()) as FeedResponse
   248→}
   249→

  1189→      paragraph(
  1190→        text(
  1191→          "Lexical is Facebook's modern text editor framework. In this guide, we'll explore how to build a feature-rich editor with custom nodes.",

  1201→
  1202→      heading('h2', text('Core Concepts')),
  1203→
  1204→      paragraph(text('Lexical operates on three core concepts:')),

  1214→            text('Immutable snapshot of your editor content'),
  1215→          ),
  1216→          paragraph(
  1217→            text('Nodes', FORMAT_BOLD),
  1218→            { type: 'linebreak', version: 1 } as any,
  1219→            text('Atomic units representing content'),
  1220→          ),
  1221→        ],
  1222→        direction: 'ltr',

  1225→        version: 1,

  1228→      heading('h2', text('Creating Custom Nodes')),
  1229→
  1230→      paragraph(text("Here's how to create a custom node:")),
  1231→
  1232→      {
  1233→        type: 'code-block',
  1234→        language: 'typescript',
  1235→        code: `export class CustomNode extends DecoratorNode<ReactElement> {
  1236→  static getType(): string {
  1237→    return 'custom'
  1238→  }
  1239→
  1240→  createDOM(): HTMLElement {
  1241→    return document.createElement('div')
  1242→  }
  1243→
  1244→  decorate(): ReactElement {
  1245→    return <CustomRenderer />
  1246→  }
  1247→}`,
  1248→        version: 1,
  1249→      } as any,
  1250→
  1251→      alertQuote(

     1→import 'modern-normalize/modern-normalize.css'
     2→import '@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/style.css'
     3→import '@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/style-renderer.css'
     4→import 'tldraw/tldraw.css'
     5→import './demo.css'
     6→
     7→import type { ColorScheme } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     8→import { DialogStackProvider } from '@haklex/rich-editor-ui'
     9→import { TldrawConfigProvider } from '@haklex/rich-renderers/tldraw'
    10→import { Monitor, Moon, Sun } from 'lucide-react'
    11→import { useCallback, useEffect, useMemo, useState } from 'react'
    12→
    13→import { ThemeContext } from './context/ThemeContext'
    14→import { DiffPage } from './pages/DiffPage'
    15→import { EditorPage } from './pages/EditorPage'
    16→import { NodeShowcase } from './pages/NodeShowcase'
    17→import { PresetsPage } from './pages/PresetsPage'
    18→
    19→type Page = 'editor' | 'nodes' | 'presets' | 'diff'
    20→type ThemeMode = 'system' | 'light' | 'dark'
    21→
    22→function useSystemColorScheme(): ColorScheme {
    23→  const [scheme, setScheme] = useState<ColorScheme>(() =>
    24→    window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches
    25→      ? 'dark'
    26→      : 'light',
    27→  )
    28→
    29→  useEffect(() => {
    30→    const mq = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)')
    31→    const handler = (e: MediaQueryListEvent) =>
    32→      setScheme(e.matches ? 'dark' : 'light')
    33→    mq.addEventListener('change', handler)
    34→    return () => mq.removeEventListener('change', handler)
    35→  }, [])
    36→
    37→  return scheme
    38→}
    39→
    40→export function App() {
    41→  const [currentPage, setCurrentPage] = useState<Page>(() => {
    42→    const hash = window.location.hash.slice(1) as Page
    43→    return hash === 'nodes' || hash === 'presets' || hash === 'diff'
    44→      ? hash
    45→      : 'editor'
    46→  })
    47→  const [themeMode, setThemeMode] = useState<ThemeMode>('system')
    48→  const systemScheme = useSystemColorScheme()
    49→
    50→  const resolved: ColorScheme =
    51→    themeMode === 'system' ? systemScheme : themeMode
    52→
    53→  const dataTheme = useMemo(
    54→    () => (themeMode === 'system' ? systemScheme : themeMode),
    55→    [systemScheme, themeMode],
    56→  )
    57→
    58→  const cycleTheme = useCallback(() => {
    59→    setThemeMode((m) => {
    60→      if (m === 'system') return 'light'
    61→      if (m === 'light') return 'dark'
    62→      return 'system'
    63→    })
    64→  }, [])
    65→
    66→  const themeLabel =
    67→    themeMode === 'system' ? 'System' : themeMode === 'light' ? 'Light' : 'Dark'
    68→  const ThemeIcon =
    69→    themeMode === 'system' ? Monitor : themeMode === 'light' ? Sun : Moon
    70→
    71→  useEffect(() => {
    72→    const handleHashChange = () => {
    73→      const hash = window.location.hash.slice(1) as Page
    74→      const validPage =
    75→        hash === 'nodes' || hash === 'presets' || hash === 'diff'
    76→          ? hash
    77→          : 'editor'
    78→      setCurrentPage(validPage)
    79→    }
    80→
    81→    window.addEventListener('hashchange', handleHashChange)
    82→    return () => window.removeEventListener('hashchange', handleHashChange)
    83→  }, [])
    84→
    85→  const navigate = (page: Page) => {
    86→    window.location.hash = page
    87→  }
    88→
    89→  const mockSaveSnapshot = useCallback(async (snapshot: object) => {
    90→    const json = JSON.stringify(snapshot)
    91→    const blob = new Blob([json], { type: 'application/json' })
    92→    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob)
    93→    console.log('[TldrawConfig] saveSnapshot → blob URL:', url)
    94→    return url
    95→  }, [])
    96→
    97→  return (
    98→    <ThemeContext value={resolved}>
    99→      <TldrawConfigProvider saveSnapshot={mockSaveSnapshot}>
   100→        <DialogStackProvider>
   101→          <div className="app" data-theme={dataTheme}>
   102→            <header className="app-header">
   103→              <div className="app-header-content">
   104→                <div>
   105→                  <h1 className="app-title">@haklex/rich-editor</h1>
   106→                  <p className="app-subtitle">
   107→                    Lexical-based rich text editor & renderer
   108→                  </p>
   109→                </div>
   110→                <nav className="app-nav">
   111→                  <button
   112→                    className={
   113→                      currentPage === 'editor'
   114→                        ? 'nav-tab nav-tab-active'
   115→                        : 'nav-tab'
   116→                    }
   117→                    onClick={() => navigate('editor')}
   118→                  >
   119→                    Editor
   120→                  </button>
   121→                  <button
   122→                    className={
   123→                      currentPage === 'nodes'
   124→                        ? 'nav-tab nav-tab-active'
   125→                        : 'nav-tab'
   126→                    }
   127→                    onClick={() => navigate('nodes')}
   128→                  >
   129→                    Node Showcase
   130→                  </button>
   131→                  <button
   132→                    className={
   133→                      currentPage === 'presets'
   134→                        ? 'nav-tab nav-tab-active'
   135→                        : 'nav-tab'
   136→                    }
   137→                    onClick={() => navigate('presets')}
   138→                  >
   139→                    Presets
   140→                  </button>
   141→                  <button
   142→                    className={
   143→                      currentPage === 'diff'
   144→                        ? 'nav-tab nav-tab-active'
   145→                        : 'nav-tab'
   146→                    }
   147→                    onClick={() => navigate('diff')}
   148→                  >
   149→                    Diff
   150→                  </button>
   151→                  <div className="nav-divider" />
   152→                  <button
   153→                    className="nav-theme-toggle"
   154→                    onClick={cycleTheme}
   155→                    title={themeLabel}
   156→                  >
   157→                    <ThemeIcon size={18} />
   158→                  </button>
   159→                </nav>
   160→              </div>
   161→            </header>
   162→
   163→            <main className="app-main">
   164→              {currentPage === 'editor' && <EditorPage />}
   165→              {currentPage === 'nodes' && <NodeShowcase />}
   166→              {currentPage === 'presets' && <PresetsPage />}
   167→              {currentPage === 'diff' && <DiffPage />}
   168→            </main>
   169→          </div>
   170→        </DialogStackProvider>
   171→      </TldrawConfigProvider>
   172→    </ThemeContext>
   173→  )
   174→}
   175→
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'
     2→
     3→import { App } from './App'
     4→
     5→window.addEventListener('error', (e) => {
     6→  document.getElementById('root')!.innerHTML =
     7→    `<pre style="color:red;padding:20px;white-space:pre-wrap">${e.message}\n\n${e.error?.stack || ''}</pre>`
     8→})
     9→
    10→try {
    11→  createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!).render(<App />)
    12→} catch (e: unknown) {
    13→  const err = e as Error
    14→  document.getElementById('root')!.innerHTML =
    15→    `<pre style="color:red;padding:20px;white-space:pre-wrap">${err.message}\n\n${err.stack || ''}</pre>`
    16→}
    17→

     1→import type { SerializedEditorState, SerializedLexicalNode } from 'lexical'
     2→
     3→// Re-export common node types (defined inline to avoid Lexical package import issues)
     4→type SerializedTextNode = SerializedLexicalNode & {
     5→  type: 'text'
     6→  text: string
     7→  format: number
     8→  detail: number
     9→  mode: 'normal' | 'token' | 'segmented'
    10→  style: string
    11→}
    12→
    13→type SerializedParagraphNode = SerializedLexicalNode & {
    14→  type: 'paragraph'
    15→  children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
    16→  direction: 'ltr' | 'rtl' | null
    17→  format: string
    18→  indent: number
    19→  textFormat: number
    20→  textStyle: string
    21→}
    22→
    23→type SerializedHeadingNode = SerializedLexicalNode & {
    24→  type: 'heading'
    25→  tag: 'h1' | 'h2' | 'h3' | 'h4' | 'h5' | 'h6'
    26→  children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
    27→  direction: 'ltr' | 'rtl' | null
    28→  format: string
    29→  indent: number
    30→}
    31→
    32→type SerializedQuoteNode = SerializedLexicalNode & {
    33→  type: 'quote'
    34→  children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
    35→  direction: 'ltr' | 'rtl' | null
    36→  format: string
    37→  indent: number
    38→}
    39→
    40→type SerializedListNode = SerializedLexicalNode & {
    41→  type: 'list'
    42→  listType: 'bullet' | 'number'
    43→  start: number
    44→  tag: 'ul' | 'ol'
    45→  children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
    46→  direction: 'ltr' | 'rtl' | null
    47→  format: string
    48→  indent: number
    49→}
    50→
    51→type SerializedListItemNode = SerializedLexicalNode & {
    52→  type: 'listitem'
    53→  value: number
    54→  children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
    55→  direction: 'ltr' | 'rtl' | null
    56→  format: string
    57→  indent: number
    58→}
    59→
    60→type SerializedLinkNode = SerializedLexicalNode & {
    61→  type: 'link'
    62→  url: string
    63→  rel: string | null
    64→  target: string | null
    65→  title: string | null
    66→  children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
    67→  direction: 'ltr' | 'rtl' | null
    68→  format: string
    69→  indent: number
    70→}
    71→
    72→// Text formats (bitwise flags)
    73→export const FORMAT_BOLD = 1
    74→export const FORMAT_ITALIC = 1 << 1
    75→export const FORMAT_STRIKETHROUGH = 1 << 2
    76→export const FORMAT_UNDERLINE = 1 << 3
    77→export const FORMAT_CODE = 1 << 4
    78→export const FORMAT_SUBSCRIPT = 1 << 5
    79→export const FORMAT_SUPERSCRIPT = 1 << 6
    80→export const FORMAT_HIGHLIGHT = 1 << 7
    81→
    82→export function text(content: string, format = 0): SerializedTextNode {
    83→  return {
    84→    detail: 0,
    85→    format,
    86→    mode: 'normal',
    87→    style: '',
    88→    text: content,
    89→    type: 'text',
    90→    version: 1,
    91→  }
    92→}
    93→
    94→export function paragraph(
    95→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
    96→): SerializedParagraphNode {
    97→  return {
    98→    children,
    99→    direction: 'ltr',
   100→    format: '',
   101→    indent: 0,
   102→    type: 'paragraph',
   103→    version: 1,
   104→    textFormat: 0,
   105→    textStyle: '',
   106→  }
   107→}
   108→
   109→export function heading(
   110→  tag: 'h1' | 'h2' | 'h3' | 'h4' | 'h5' | 'h6',
   111→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
   112→): SerializedHeadingNode {
   113→  return {
   114→    children,
   115→    direction: 'ltr',
   116→    format: '',
   117→    indent: 0,
   118→    tag,
   119→    type: 'heading',
   120→    version: 1,
   121→  }
   122→}
   123→
   124→export function quote(
   125→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
   126→): SerializedQuoteNode {
   127→  return {
   128→    children,
   129→    direction: 'ltr',
   130→    format: '',
   131→    indent: 0,
   132→    type: 'quote',
   133→    version: 1,
   134→  }
   135→}
   136→
   137→export function list(
   138→  listType: 'bullet' | 'number',
   139→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
   140→): SerializedListNode {
   141→  return {
   142→    children,
   143→    direction: 'ltr',
   144→    format: '',
   145→    indent: 0,
   146→    listType,
   147→    start: 1,
   148→    tag: listType === 'bullet' ? 'ul' : 'ol',
   149→    type: 'list',
   150→    version: 1,
   151→  }
   152→}
   153→
   154→export function listItem(
   155→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
   156→): SerializedListItemNode {
   157→  return {
   158→    children,
   159→    direction: 'ltr',
   160→    format: '',
   161→    indent: 0,
   162→    type: 'listitem',
   163→    version: 1,
   164→    value: 1,
   165→  }
   166→}
   167→
   168→export function link(
   169→  url: string,
   170→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
   171→): SerializedLinkNode {
   172→  return {
   173→    children,
   174→    direction: 'ltr',
   175→    format: '',
   176→    indent: 0,
   177→    type: 'link',
   178→    version: 1,
   179→    rel: null,
   180→    target: null,
   181→    title: null,
   182→    url,
   183→  }
   184→}
   185→
   186→export function horizontalRule() {
   187→  return {
   188→    type: 'horizontalrule',
   189→    version: 1,
   190→  }
   191→}
   192→
   193→export function lineBreak() {
   194→  return {
   195→    type: 'linebreak',
   196→    version: 1,
   197→  }
   198→}
   199→
   200→export function table(...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]) {
   201→  return {
   202→    children,
   203→    direction: 'ltr' as const,
   204→    format: '',
   205→    indent: 0,
   206→    type: 'table',
   207→    version: 1,
   208→  }
   209→}
   210→
   211→export function tableRow(...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]) {
   212→  return {
   213→    children,
   214→    direction: 'ltr' as const,
   215→    format: '',
   216→    indent: 0,
   217→    type: 'tablerow',
   218→    version: 1,
   219→  }
   220→}
   221→
   222→export function tableCell(
   223→  headerState: number,
   224→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
   225→) {
   226→  return {
   227→    children,
   228→    colSpan: 1,
   229→    direction: 'ltr' as const,
   230→    format: '',
   231→    headerState,
   232→    indent: 0,
   233→    type: 'tablecell',
   234→    version: 1,
   235→    width: null,
   236→  }
   237→}
   238→
   239→export function alertQuote(
   240→  alertType: string,
   241→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
   242→) {
   243→  return {
   244→    type: 'alert-quote',
   245→    alertType,
   246→    content: {
   247→      root: {
   248→        children,
   249→        direction: 'ltr' as const,
   250→        format: '',
   251→        indent: 0,
   252→        type: 'root' as const,
   253→        version: 1,
   254→      },
   255→    },
   256→    version: 1,
   257→  }
   258→}
   259→
   260→export function image(payload: {
   261→  src: string
   262→  altText: string
   263→  width?: number
   264→  height?: number
   265→  caption?: string
   266→  thumbhash?: string
   267→  accent?: string
   268→}) {
   269→  return {
   270→    type: 'image',
   271→    src: payload.src,
   272→    altText: payload.altText,
   273→    width: payload.width,
   274→    height: payload.height,
   275→    caption: payload.caption,
   276→    thumbhash: payload.thumbhash,
   277→    accent: payload.accent,
   278→    version: 1,
   279→  }
   280→}
   281→
   282→export function video(payload: {
   283→  src: string
   284→  poster?: string
   285→  width?: number
   286→  height?: number
   287→}) {
   288→  return {
   289→    type: 'video',

   293→    height: payload.height,
   294→    version: 1,
   295→  }
   296→}
   297→
   298→export function mermaid(diagram: string) {
   299→  return {

   306→export function katexBlock(equation: string) {

   314→export function katexInline(equation: string) {

   322→export function details(

   338→
   339→export function spoiler(...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]) {

   349→
   350→export function footnote(identifier: string) {

   352→    type: 'footnote',

   356→}
   357→
   358→export function footnoteSection(definitions: Record<string, string>) {
   359→  return {
   360→    type: 'footnote-section',
   361→    definitions,
   362→    version: 1,
   363→  }
   364→}
   365→
   366→export function mention(
   367→  platform: string,
   368→  handle: string,
   369→  displayName?: string,
   370→) {
   371→  return {
   372→    type: 'mention',
   373→    platform,
   374→    handle,
   375→    ...(displayName ? { displayName } : {}),
   376→    version: 1,
   377→  }
   378→}
   379→
   380→export function banner(
   381→  bannerType: 'note' | 'tip' | 'important' | 'warning' | 'caution',
   382→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
   383→) {
   384→  return {
   385→    type: 'banner',
   386→    bannerType,
   387→    content: {
   388→      root: {
   389→        children,
   390→        direction: 'ltr' as const,
   391→        format: '',
   392→        indent: 0,
   393→        type: 'root' as const,
   394→        version: 1,
   395→      },
   396→    },
   397→    version: 1,
   398→  }
   399→}
   400→
   401→export function doc(
   402→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
   403→): SerializedEditorState {
   404→  return {
   405→    root: {
   406→      children,
   407→      direction: 'ltr',
   408→      format: '',
   409→      indent: 0,
   410→      type: 'root',
   411→      version: 1,
   412→    },
   413→  }
   414→}
   415→
```

     1→import { existsSync, readdirSync } from 'node:fs'
     2→import { resolve } from 'node:path'
     3→
     4→import { vanillaExtractPlugin } from '@vanilla-extract/vite-plugin'
     5→import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
     6→import { codeInspectorPlugin } from 'code-inspector-plugin'
     7→import type { Plugin } from 'vite'
     8→import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
     9→
    10→// Dev-only: resolve workspace style entry to source instead of dist output.
    11→// - Prefer src/style.css for plain CSS packages.
    12→// - Fall back to src/styles.css.ts for VE packages when imported from JS.
    13→// - If imported from CSS (@import), VE entries resolve to empty CSS.
    14→function watchWorkspacePlugin(): Plugin {
    15→  const packagesDir = resolve(__dirname, '..')
    16→  const srcDirs: string[] = []
    17→  for (const entry of readdirSync(packagesDir)) {
    18→    if (entry.startsWith('rich-') && entry !== 'rich-editor-demo') {
    19→      const srcDir = resolve(packagesDir, entry, 'src')
    20→      if (existsSync(srcDir)) {
    21→        srcDirs.push(srcDir)
    22→      }
    23→    }
    24→  }
    25→
    26→  return {
    27→    name: 'watch-workspace-packages',
    28→    apply: 'serve',
    29→    configureServer(server) {
    30→      for (const dir of srcDirs) {
    31→        server.watcher.add(dir)
    32→      }
    33→      server.watcher.on('change', (file) => {
    34→        if (!srcDirs.some((dir) => file.startsWith(dir))) return
    35→        const mods = server.moduleGraph.getModulesByFile(file)
    36→        if (mods?.size) {
    37→          for (const mod of mods) server.moduleGraph.invalidateModule(mod)
    38→        }
    39→        server.hot.send({ type: 'full-reload' })
    40→      })
    41→    },
    42→  }
    43→}
    44→
    45→function workspaceCssPlugin(): Plugin {
    46→  const scopes = new Set(['@haklex', '@shiro'])
    47→  const EMPTY_PREFIX = '\0empty-css:'
    48→  const VE_PREFIX = '\0ve-css:'
    49→
    50→  return {
    51→    name: 'workspace-css-dev',
    52→    apply: 'serve',
    53→    enforce: 'pre',
    54→    resolveId(id, importer) {
    55→      const m = id.match(/^@([\w-]+)\/([\w-]+)\/style\.css$/)
    56→      if (!m) return null
    57→
    58→      const scope = `@${m[1]}`
    59→      if (!scopes.has(scope)) return null
    60→
    61→      const pkgDir = m[2]
    62→      const srcIndex = resolve(__dirname, `../${pkgDir}/src/index.ts`)
    63→      const srcCss = resolve(__dirname, `../${pkgDir}/src/style.css`)
    64→      const srcVeCss = resolve(__dirname, `../${pkgDir}/src/styles.css.ts`)
    65→
    66→      if (existsSync(srcIndex)) {
    67→        if (importer?.endsWith('.css')) {
    68→          // Keep CSS graph valid; JS graph will import source modules and emit VE CSS.
    69→          return existsSync(srcCss) ? srcCss : `${EMPTY_PREFIX}${id}`
    70→        }
    71→        return `${VE_PREFIX}${srcIndex}`
    72→      }
    73→
    74→      if (existsSync(srcCss)) {
    75→        return srcCss
    76→      }
    77→
    78→      if (!existsSync(srcVeCss)) {
    79→        return null
    80→      }
    81→
    82→      // For CSS @import, VE source cannot be parsed as CSS.
    83→      if (importer?.endsWith('.css')) {
    84→        return `${EMPTY_PREFIX}${id}`
    85→      }
    86→
    87→      // For JS imports, route style.css to VE source for real-time compilation.
    88→      return `${VE_PREFIX}${srcVeCss}`
    89→    },
    90→    load(id) {
    91→      if (id.startsWith(EMPTY_PREFIX)) return ''
    92→      if (id.startsWith(VE_PREFIX)) {
    93→        const sourcePath = id.slice(VE_PREFIX.length)
    94→        return `import ${JSON.stringify(sourcePath)}`
    95→      }
    96→    },
    97→  }
    98→}
    99→
   100→export default defineConfig({
   101→  plugins: [
   102→    watchWorkspacePlugin(),
   103→    workspaceCssPlugin(),
   104→    vanillaExtractPlugin(),
   105→    codeInspectorPlugin({ bundler: 'vite', hotKeys: ['altKey'] }),
   106→    react(),
   107→  ],
   108→  server: {
   109→    port: 5188,
   110→    open: true,
   111→  },
   112→  optimizeDeps: {
   113→    include: ['react-intersection-observer', 'react-photo-view'],
   114→  },
   115→  resolve: {
   116→    dedupe: [
   117→      'lexical',
   118→      '@lexical/code',
   119→      '@lexical/link',
   120→      '@lexical/list',
   121→      '@lexical/markdown',
   122→      '@lexical/react',
   123→      '@lexical/rich-text',
   124→      '@lexical/table',
   125→      'react',
   126→      'react-dom',
   127→      'react/jsx-runtime',
   128→
   129→      'react-intersection-observer',
   130→      'react-photo-view',
   131→    ],
   132→  },
   133→})
   134→
```

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile the complete findings.

## Summary: Presets Functionality in Rich Editor Demo

I found the preset-related functionality in the monorepo. Here's the complete overview:

### 1. **Demo Page Location**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/src/pages/PresetsPage.tsx`

This is the main component that implements the presets showcase with:
- **Layout**: Three-section layout (sidebar + main content area)
- **Sidebar**: List of presets with labels and descriptions
- **Main content**: Editor/renderer display with toolbar controls

### 2. **Preset Definition & Data**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/src/fixtures/presets.ts`

Contains the `Preset` interface and preset collection:
```typescript
interface Preset {
  key: string
  label: string
  description: string
  data: SerializedEditorState  // Lexical editor state JSON
}
```

**Current presets** (5 total):
- `note-diary` — Note variant showcase with serif font, drop cap, Chinese classical text
- `markdown-test-preset` — Markdown rendering test cases
- `tldraw-showcase` — Interactive whiteboard canvas extension demo
- `enhanced-renderers` — All renderer packages (code, image, video, linkcard, gallery, mermaid)
- `comprehensive-article` — Complete feature showcase (18+ node types)
- `tech-article` — Technical documentation with KaTeX, code blocks, alerts

### 3. **State Management**
**In PresetsPage.tsx** (lines 16-29):
```typescript
const [selectedKey, setSelectedKey] = useState(presets[0].key)  // Current preset
const [variant, setVariant] = useState<RichEditorVariant>('article')  // article|comment|note
const [mode, setMode] = useState<'edit' | 'readonly'>('edit')  // Edit vs read-only
const [liveState, setLiveState] = useState<SerializedEditorState | null>(null)
```

**Selection logic**:
- Presets are loaded from fixture file
- Selected preset found via `presets.find((p) => p.key === selectedKey)`
- When changing preset, live state resets to trigger re-render with new content

### 4. **UI Controls** (PresetsPage.tsx lines 58-96)
**Toolbar with two groups**:
- **Mode**: Edit / Readonly buttons (lines 62-73)
- **Variant**: Article / Comment / Note buttons (lines 77-95)

**Preset sidebar** (lines 36-54):
- Clickable buttons for each preset
- Active state styling via CSS class `preset-item-active`
- Shows label + description per preset

### 5. **Routing & App Integration**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/src/App.tsx`

- **Routes**: Hash-based navigation (`#editor`, `#nodes`, `#presets`, `#diff`)
- **Navigation** (line 139): "Presets" tab triggers `navigate('presets')`
- **Page state** (lines 41-46): Parsed from `window.location.hash`
- **Component mounting** (line 166): `{currentPage === 'presets' && <PresetsPage />}`

### 6. **Demo App Details**
**Structure**:
- **Location**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/`
- **Dev server**: Port 5188 (Vite)
- **Entry**: `src/main.tsx` → `src/App.tsx`
- **Pages** in `/src/pages/`:
  - `EditorPage.tsx` — Free-form editor
  - `NodeShowcase.tsx` — Individual node type demos
  - `PresetsPage.tsx` — Preset showcase (our focus)
  - `DiffPage.tsx` — Diff viewer

### 7. **Fixture Helpers**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/src/fixtures/helpers.ts`

Builder functions for constructing `SerializedEditorState`:
- `doc()`, `paragraph()`, `text()` — Basic text nodes
- `heading()`, `quote()`, `list()` — Block structures
- `alertQuote()`, `banner()`, `details()` — Special containers
- `image()`, `video()`, `mermaid()`, `katexBlock()` — Media/math
- `mention()`, `footnote()` — Advanced inline nodes
- `FORMAT_*` constants for text styling (BOLD, ITALIC, CODE, etc.)

---

**Key architectural insight**: Presets are static JSON data (Lexical editor states) that can be swapped in/out. The same component (`ShiroEditor` or `ShiroRenderer`) accepts different initial content, with UI controls for testing different variants and edit modes. This allows comprehensive feature testing in a single interface.

    17→import { PresetsPage } from './pages/PresetsPage'
    18→
    19→type Page = 'editor' | 'nodes' | 'presets' | 'diff'
    20→type ThemeMode = 'system' | 'light' | 'dark'
    21→
    22→function useSystemColorScheme(): ColorScheme {
    23→  const [scheme, setScheme] = useState<ColorScheme>(() =>
    24→    window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches
    25→      ? 'dark'
    26→      : 'light',
    27→  )
    28→
    29→  useEffect(() => {
    30→    const mq = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)')
    31→    const handler = (e: MediaQueryListEvent) =>
    32→      setScheme(e.matches ? 'dark' : 'light')
    33→    mq.addEventListener('change', handler)
    34→    return () => mq.removeEventListener('change', handler)
    35→  }, [])
    36→
    37→  return scheme
    38→}
    39→
    40→export function App() {
    41→  const [currentPage, setCurrentPage] = useState<Page>(() => {
    42→    const hash = window.location.hash.slice(1) as Page
    43→    return hash === 'nodes' || hash === 'presets' || hash === 'diff'
    44→      ? hash
    45→      : 'editor'
    46→  })
    47→  const [themeMode, setThemeMode] = useState<ThemeMode>('system')
    48→  const systemScheme = useSystemColorScheme()
    49→
    50→  const resolved: ColorScheme =
    51→    themeMode === 'system' ? systemScheme : themeMode
    52→
    53→  const dataTheme = useMemo(
    54→    () => (themeMode === 'system' ? systemScheme : themeMode),
    55→    [systemScheme, themeMode],
    56→  )
    57→
    58→  const cycleTheme = useCallback(() => {
    59→    setThemeMode((m) => {
    60→      if (m === 'system') return 'light'
    61→      if (m === 'light') return 'dark'
    62→      return 'system'
    63→    })
    64→  }, [])
    65→
    66→  const themeLabel =
    67→    themeMode === 'system' ? 'System' : themeMode === 'light' ? 'Light' : 'Dark'
    68→  const ThemeIcon =
    69→    themeMode === 'system' ? Monitor : themeMode === 'light' ? Sun : Moon
    70→
    71→  useEffect(() => {
    72→    const handleHashChange = () => {
    73→      const hash = window.location.hash.slice(1) as Page
    74→      const validPage =
    75→        hash === 'nodes' || hash === 'presets' || hash === 'diff'
    76→          ? hash
    77→          : 'editor'
    78→      setCurrentPage(validPage)
    79→    }
    80→
    81→    window.addEventListener('hashchange', handleHashChange)
    82→    return () => window.removeEventListener('hashchange', handleHashChange)
    83→  }, [])
    84→
    85→  const navigate = (page: Page) => {
    86→    window.location.hash = page
    87→  }
    88→
    89→  const mockSaveSnapshot = useCallback(async (snapshot: object) => {
    90→    const json = JSON.stringify(snapshot)
    91→    const blob = new Blob([json], { type: 'application/json' })
    92→    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob)
    93→    console.log('[TldrawConfig] saveSnapshot → blob URL:', url)
    94→    return url
    95→  }, [])
    96→
    97→  return (
    98→    <ThemeContext value={resolved}>
    99→      <TldrawConfigProvider saveSnapshot={mockSaveSnapshot}>
   100→        <DialogStackProvider>
   101→          <div className="app" data-theme={dataTheme}>
   102→            <header className="app-header">
   103→              <div className="app-header-content">
   104→                <div>
   105→                  <h1 className="app-title">@haklex/rich-editor</h1>
   106→                  <p className="app-subtitle">
   107→                    Lexical-based rich text editor & renderer
   108→                  </p>
   109→                </div>
   110→                <nav className="app-nav">
   111→                  <button
   112→                    className={
   113→                      currentPage === 'editor'
   114→                        ? 'nav-tab nav-tab-active'
   115→                        : 'nav-tab'
   116→                    }
   117→                    onClick={() => navigate('editor')}
   118→                  >
   119→                    Editor
   120→                  </button>
   121→                  <button
   122→                    className={
   123→                      currentPage === 'nodes'
   124→                        ? 'nav-tab nav-tab-active'
   125→                        : 'nav-tab'
   126→                    }
   127→                    onClick={() => navigate('nodes')}
   128→                  >
   129→                    Node Showcase
   130→                  </button>
   131→                  <button
   132→                    className={
   133→                      currentPage === 'presets'
   134→                        ? 'nav-tab nav-tab-active'
   135→                        : 'nav-tab'
   136→                    }
   137→                    onClick={() => navigate('presets')}
   138→                  >
   139→                    Presets
   140→                  </button>
   141→                  <button
   142→                    className={
   143→                      currentPage === 'diff'
   144→                        ? 'nav-tab nav-tab-active'
   145→                        : 'nav-tab'
   146→                    }
   147→                    onClick={() => navigate('diff')}
   148→                  >
   149→                    Diff
   150→                  </button>
   151→                  <div className="nav-divider" />
   152→                  <button
   153→                    className="nav-theme-toggle"
   154→                    onClick={cycleTheme}
   155→                    title={themeLabel}
   156→                  >
   157→                    <ThemeIcon size={18} />
   158→                  </button>
   159→                </nav>
   160→              </div>
   161→            </header>
   162→
   163→            <main className="app-main">
   164→              {currentPage === 'editor' && <EditorPage />}
   165→              {currentPage === 'nodes' && <NodeShowcase />}
   166→              {currentPage === 'presets' && <PresetsPage />}
   167→              {currentPage === 'diff' && <DiffPage />}
   168→            </main>
   169→          </div>
   170→        </DialogStackProvider>
   171→      </TldrawConfigProvider>
   172→    </ThemeContext>
   173→  )
   174→}
   175→
```
</details></tool-use>
